perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Provided that the Recipient has a written agreement with the following persons or entities requiring them to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement, the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to: 2.3.1 Any other party with the Discloser’s prior written consent; and 2.3.2 the Recipient’s employees, of ficials, representatives and agents who have a strict need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , and employees , officials, representatives and agents of any legal entity that it controls, controls it, or with which it is under common contro l, who have a similar need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , provided that, for these purposes a controlled legal entity means: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over fift y percent (50%) of voting shares thereof; or, 2.3.2.2 any entity over which the Party exercises effective managerial control; or,
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to: 2.3.1 Any other party with the Discloser’s prior written consent; and 2.3.2 the Recipient’s employees, of ficials, representatives and agents who have a strict need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , and employees , officials, representatives and agents of any legal entity that it controls, controls it, or with which it is under common contro l, who have a similar need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , provided that, for these purposes a controlled legal entity means: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over fift y percent (50%) of voting shares thereof; or, 2.3.2.2 any entity over which the Party exercises effective managerial control; or,
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the 'Provided that the Recipient has a written agreement with the following persons or entities requiring them to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement' clause eliminates the requirement for a written agreement to protect confidential information when disclosing it to third parties or employees. This contradicts section 2.1, which obligates the Recipient to protect the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate ;
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for a written agreement to protect confidential information when disclosing it. Section 2.1 states that 'The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure...'. By removing the written agreement requirement, the recipient can now disclose information without needing to ensure the same level of protection as outlined in section 2.1, creating a direct contradiction. This is a strong contradiction as it directly impacts the level of care and discretion the recipient must exercise.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The Recipient agrees to indem nify UNHCR in respect of any expenses, losses, damages, costs, claims or liability UNHCR may suffer or incur as a result of an act or omission by the Recipient or its 4 employees, consultants and agents in connection with the Confidential Information and the Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The Recipient agrees to indem nify UNHCR in respect of any expenses, losses, damages, costs, claims or liability UNHCR may suffer or incur as a result of an intentional act by the Recipient in connection with the Confidential Information and the Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'act or omission' to 'intentional act,' the Recipient is no longer liable for unintentional breaches of confidentiality. This reduction in liability contradicts section 2.1 and 2.2, which creates an obligation to the Recipient to use Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed and with discretion. Reducing the scope of indemnity weakens the protection offered to UNHCR.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate ;
perturbation.justification: The original text held the Recipient liable for 'any expenses, losses, damages... as a result of an act or omission'. The changed text narrows this liability to only 'intentional act'. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which requires the Recipient to use 'the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure... as it uses with its own similar information'. By limiting liability to intentional acts, unintentional disclosures (resulting from a lack of 'care and discretion') are no longer covered, creating a clear contradiction in the obligations and protections afforded to UNHCR as it relates to Confidential Information.
